pr.title: if: Match ifstatus and partially match if_2FC93
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched ifStatus_802F5E50 at 100% and partially improved un_802FD928 to 99.2% in the interface/HUD-related code. The PR replaced a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with a small helper that finds an IfDamageState by its next HSD_GObj pointer and conditionally calls HSD_GObj_JObjCallback when hide_all_digits is not set. It also adjusted un_802FD928 control flow and argument typing for better matching, added a named local struct tag for the static un_804A1F10 data, updated matching status in scratches.txt, and corrected headers to reflect the new function signatures.
what_changed: Added config/GALE01/scratches.txt entry marking ifStatus_802F5E50 as 100%:MATCHED by itsgrimetime.
Implemented ifStatus_802F5E50 in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c instead of leaving it as NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
Changed ifStatus_802F5E50 signature from void(void) to void(HSD_GObj* gobj, s32 arg1) in both source and header.
Added static inline helper getPlayerByNext(HSD_GObj* gobj), which scans ifStatus_HudInfo.players[0..5] for a matching next pointer and returns the corresponding IfDamageState.
ifStatus_802F5E50 now looks up the player damage state and calls HSD_GObj_JObjCallback(gobj, arg1) only when player->flags.hide_all_digits is false.
Named the previously anonymous static bss struct in if_2FC93.c as struct un_804A1F10_t.
Changed un_802FD928 parameter arg1 from int to u8 in source and header.
Reworked un_802FD928 from nested conditionals into early-return and continue-based control flow, including a temporary pointer to un_804A1F10, to improve matching.
un_802FD928 still filters out slot values >= 4, CPU player slots, already-used x28 entries, and already-marked x24 slots before storing slot, arg1, and GXColor data.
smart_moves: Kept the public header signatures synchronized with matched source changes for both ifStatus_802F5E50 and un_802FD928.
Used a narrow u8 type for un_802FD928 arg1, matching how the value is stored in un_804A1F10.x28 and improving type accuracy.
Introduced a struct tag for un_804A1F10_t so the static global layout could be referenced through a typed local pointer during matching work.
Split the ifStatus_802F5E50 behavior into a small lookup helper plus the matched callback wrapper, making the matched logic easier to read and reuse.
Recorded the completed ifStatus_802F5E50 match in scratches.txt, preserving project-wide match tracking.
decomp_lessons: When a function is newly matched, update both the source implementation and any prototypes in headers; stale void(void) prototypes can mask real call signatures.
Small type refinements, such as int to u8, can be necessary for matching and also document the real storage width.
For matching, restructuring equivalent logic into early returns and continues can produce closer codegen than compact nested conditionals.
Adding a tag name to an otherwise anonymous static struct can enable pointer temporaries that help match generated assembly while preserving the same storage layout.
Avoid quick symbol/linking hacks that confuse matching/nonmatching boundaries; reviewer feedback showed that macro-renaming or ad hoc symbol fixes are not acceptable cleanup substitutes.
If a helper returns NULL in theory, callers should be reviewed for whether original code assumes success; this PR evidence shows no explicit NULL guard in ifStatus_802F5E50.
naming_conventions: Matched function kept address-based project naming: ifStatus_802F5E50.
Unknown interface function retained un_ address naming: un_802FD928.
Static bss struct tag used address-style naming: un_804A1F10_t.
Local helper used descriptive camelCase-style name getPlayerByNext based on the field it searches, ifStatus_HudInfo.players[i].next.
HUD damage state fields used semantic names visible in diff: IfDamageState, ifStatus_HudInfo, flags.hide_all_digits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed un_802FD928 control flow from one outer slot check plus nested conditions to an early slot >= 4 return and a for-loop with continue statements.
Introduced a local pointer temporary, struct un_804A1F10_t* tmp = &un_804A1F10, and used it for the x28 check while leaving later stores through un_804A1F10.
Changed arg1 type to u8 for un_802FD928, likely affecting register/value handling and store behavior for the partial 99.2% match.
Implemented ifStatus_802F5E50 as a direct lookup followed by a conditional HSD_GObj_JObjCallback call, replacing a stub to reach 100% match.
A rejected/intermediate approach shown in review involved '#define ifStatus_802F5E50 ifStatus_802F5E50_' to address linking/matching confusion; this was called out and cleaned up.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.context: Reviewer objected to an odd hunk that included getPlayerByNext plus a macro '#define ifStatus_802F5E50 ifStatus_802F5E50_'.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.comment: sorry about that - i'll clean this up. claude was confused about matching/nonmatching file and symbols.txt and I just asked it to "fix the linking errors" like a moron.
review_feedback.context: Author acknowledged the problematic linking/symbol workaround and indicated it would be cleaned up.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match un_802FD928 or rename if_2FC93 symbols.
Search for other uses of ifStatus_802F5E50 to confirm the HSD_GObj* and s32 callback signature.
Inspect whether getPlayerByNext or equivalent player-next lookup helpers appear elsewhere in ifstatus code.
Check subsequent reviews for cleanup of AI-generated matching/linking workarounds involving symbols.txt or nonmatching files.
Look for later struct renames or field identification for un_804A1F10_t.
